---
title: "Gas Exchange at the Alveoli"
author: "Kelly Purdy"
date: ""
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    transition: fade
    center: true
    slide-number: false
---

## Gas Exchange at the Alveoli  
### Anatomy & Physiology

::: notes
Today I will explain how gas exchange occurs in the lungs, specifically at the alveoli.
:::

---

## What Are Alveoli?

::: fragment
- Microscopic air sacs in the lungs
:::
::: fragment
- Located at the ends of bronchioles
:::
::: fragment
- Primary site of gas exchange
:::

![](https://9b6a32161ce9436fb335be90283dfa18.app.posit.cloud/file_show?path=%2Fcloud%2Fproject%2FPicture+1.jpeg)

:::

::: notes
Alveoli are tiny air sacs found at the ends of bronchioles.
They are the main location where oxygen and carbon dioxide are exchanged between air and blood.
:::

---

## Structure Supports Function

::: fragment
- Walls are one cell thick
:::
::: fragment
- Surrounded by capillaries
:::
::: fragment
- Large surface area
:::

::: notes
The structure of alveoli makes gas exchange efficient.
Thin walls reduce diffusion distance, and capillaries bring blood very close to the air.
:::

---

## How Gas Exchange Occurs

![](https://9b6a32161ce9436fb335be90283dfa18.app.posit.cloud/file_show?path=%2Fcloud%2Fproject%2FPicture+2.jpeg)

::: fragment
- Oxygen diffuses into the blood
:::
::: fragment
- Carbon dioxide diffuses into the alveoli
:::
::: fragment
- Movement occurs by diffusion
:::

::: notes
Oxygen moves from the alveoli into the blood because its concentration is higher in the alveoli.
Carbon dioxide moves from the blood into the alveoli.
This process happens by diffusion.
:::

---

## Why Alveoli Are Efficient

::: fragment
- Short diffusion distance
:::
::: fragment
- Moist surface helps gases dissolve
:::
::: fragment
- Constant blood flow maintains gradients
:::

::: notes
These features allow gas exchange to happen quickly and continuously.
Blood flow maintains concentration gradients so diffusion can keep occurring.
:::

---

## Summary

::: fragment
- Alveoli are the functional units of the lungs
:::
::: fragment
- Specialized anatomy supports diffusion
:::
::: fragment
- Gas exchange is essential for cellular respiration
:::

::: notes
In summary, alveoli are specialized structures that allow oxygen to enter the bloodstream and carbon dioxide to be removed.
This process is essential for cellular respiration and life.
:::
